body_txt: The Female Gaze is a Gaze trope about the way a work is presented from a female perspective or reflects female attitudes, either because of the creator's gender or because it is deliberately aimed at a female audience. Although looking at sexy men can contribute to it, Female Gaze is not restricted to that but is more importantly about the expectations of how the (presumptive) audience relate to the work. As a feminist media theory, the idea goes further and suggests an outlook where neither party is given a predominant importance. The usual result of Female Gaze leads to a focus on the buttocks, groin, chest, abs, biceps, and similar examples of male primary and secondary sex characteristics. This trope can be used as a legitimate cinematic effect , especially when combined with Point of View . (At that point it may become Eating the Eye Candy , though not necessarily.) Female Gaze does sometimes focus on female attributes, such as bust line and hips, but this is more likely to be used as an Establishing Character Moment such as the Bitch Alert , which can manifest as a POV shot panning from her low accentuated bustline to her icy gaze, both meant to intimidate her female peers. Alternatively or additionally, shots or a pan of her feet, hips, butt and chest can be used to display her arrogant strut as a means of establishing her arrogant and domineering personality. However, female viewers can still get aroused/aspirational when the female characters appear in Stripperiffic gear and have large breasts (sometime small breasts ). Female Gaze is often used as a Distaff Counterpart to Male Gaze . It is generally less common than its Spear Counterpart , and this is largely believed to be because Most Writers Are Male . May overlap with Homosexual Male Gaze . Compare Eating the Eye Candy and Longing Look .
